title: How eXp Realty Agent Resources Are Structured
excerpt: eXp Realty agent resources help you work smarter, faster, and grow stronger. Learn how this agent-first brokerage keeps leading the industry.
content:
Key Takeaway: Innovation at eXp Realty is not marketing language, it is an operating system. Because the brokerage is profitable and virtual by design, it continuously reinvests in agent resources, technology, and infrastructure that help agents work more efficiently without increasing fees or adding friction.

TL;DR About eXp Realty Innovation and Agent Resources

  • eXp Realty reinvests brokerage profits directly into agent tools and systems
  • Innovation is driven by a cloud-based model, not physical office overhead
  • New agent resources are released regularly, with major launches at EXPCON
  • Technology is designed to simplify workflows, not add platforms
  • Agent resources support productivity, marketing, analytics, and collaboration
  • Innovation functions as part of eXp’s broader agent support ecosystem

Most brokerages treat innovation as a one-time upgrade. At eXp Realty, innovation is ongoing infrastructure. Because the company operates virtually and remains consistently profitable, it can reinvest in agent resources that evolve with the market instead of reacting after agents feel the pain.

This article explains how innovation and agent resources fit into the broader eXp Realty support ecosystem available to eXp agents. Here’s your handy dandy index:

Table of Contents

TL;DR About eXp Realty Innovation and Agent Resources
Why eXp Realty Stays Ahead of the Pack
Constantly Evolving: eXp’s New Resources and Innovations
How Does eXp Realty Keep Raising the Bar?
The eXp Business Model in a Nutshell
What Agents Also Ask About eXp Realty Innovation
Why This Matters Before You Join eXp Realty
Frequently Asked Questions
Related eXp Realty Support Guides

Why eXp Realty Stays Ahead of the Pack

Through its innovative, agent-first model, eXp Realty has reshaped what a real estate brokerage can be and became the brokerage that serves more families than any other. This isn’t just some rebranded franchise with a new logo—it’s a full-blown evolution of the industry. By putting agents at the center of every decision, eXp has flipped the traditional brokerage pyramid upside down. The result? A business model that runs on agent empowerment, profitability, and relentless modernization.

This agent-centric design gives eXp agents access to a steady stream of real estate agent resources, tools, and support that actually keep up with the pace of the industry. Where most brokerages play catch-up, eXp is leading the charge. From automated marketing to productivity dashboards and virtual real estate workspaces, the company’s cloud-based infrastructure means agents can scale smarter and faster—without the baggage of outdated tech or expensive office overhead.

Let’s talk about innovation real estate agents can actually feel. eXp Realty doesn’t just slap “tech-forward” on a flyer—it shows up with the goods. Whether it’s interactive listing tools, real-time CRM integrations, or AI-powered lead systems, every piece of technology for real estate is designed to help agents grow their business—not just survive market shifts. No more juggling platforms that don’t talk to each other. No more hours wasted on manual follow-up. Just smooth, integrated systems that make it easier to do your job and look good doing it.

And guess what? That kind of innovation directly boosts client service, too. When you’ve got tools like virtual walkthroughs, branded buyer/seller kits, and live communication links at your fingertips, your buyers and sellers feel it. They trust it. That confidence leads to faster deals, better referrals, and more long-term wins.

Constantly Evolving: eXp’s New Resources and Innovations

Most brokerages stick with the same old tools year after year. eXp Realty? It rolls out new resources consistently, keeping agents ahead of the curve. Sure, there are updates throughout the year, but it’s at EXPCON—eXp’s largest annual in-person event—where you can expect a game-changing lineup of announcements. These aren’t just surface-level tweaks. We’re talking real estate agent resources that directly enhance productivity, boost marketing efforts, and drive profitability, all tailored to help eXp agents work smarter and grow faster.

In 2024, eXp Realty came out swinging. At EXPCON Miami, they revealed the acquisition of luxury marketing platform LUXVT, added full agent access to Canva Pro (Enterprise level, loaded with pre-built eXp templates), rolled out a sleek new public website, and announced a powerful partnership with Sisu—a business intelligence platform that helps agents track performance and streamline operations.

2023 was just as stacked. That year, eXp launched eXp Exclusives, giving agents and their clients first dibs on pre-market and off-market listings. The Listing Presentation Kit and Active Listing Kit debuted as customizable, high-impact marketing tools. eXp World moved from a clunky download to a browser-based experience, and agents gained access to My Link My Lead, a tool that turns content into clickable lead funnels.

Other recent innovations include the launch of eXp Luxury, SUCCESS® Coaching, and SUCCESS® Lending, plus the expansion of the Revenos referral network and the debut of SUCCESS Health. These aren’t just shiny extras—they’re built-in tools tied directly to eXp Realty agent success, showing exactly why the eXp business model is in a league of its own.

How Does eXp Realty Keep Raising the Bar?

Let’s talk money—specifically eXp profits, and how they’re put to work. Unlike many brokerages still patching holes in a sinking ship, eXp Realty is not just surviving—it’s profitable. Very profitable! And instead of hoarding those profits, eXp reinvests them directly into agent resources that actually move the needle. We’re talking upgraded technology for real estate, cutting-edge marketing tools, and advanced training platforms that help eXp agents work smarter, not harder.

That sustainable, reinvest-and-grow model isn’t just smart—it’s empowering. It creates long-term growth, better eXp agent support, and a system that evolves with the market instead of reacting to it ten steps too late. This is what agent-focused innovation actually looks like in practice, and it’s one of the biggest eXp Realty benefits there is.

Now, how can eXp afford to outpace every other brokerage when it comes to real estate agent resources? Easy: it’s the only publicly-traded real estate brokerage that’s been profitable for the past five years. Yep, the only one! That’s a big deal because when brokerages bleed money, guess who ends up covering the costs? You. Those surprise “admin fees,” downgraded tools, or mysterious commission split changes? That’s not bad luck—it’s a business model in crisis.

Unprofitable brokerages cut corners, easy to see when you read anonymous agent reviews. Those brokerages stall on tech upgrades. They lay off support teams. They rebrand, restructure, or vanish altogether. And somewhere in the middle of a transaction, you’re the one left explaining it to a confused client. That’s not just annoying—it’s a hit to your credibility and your paycheck.

The most successful real estate companies don’t just weather tough markets—they prepare for them. They manage expenses, leverage smart systems, and protect their agents’ bottom line. So if you’ve ever Googled “is eXp Realty profitable?” or “how to value a real estate brokerage”, here’s your answer: the value of eXp lies in its sustainability because broke brokerages break agents. eXp is built to keep you winning.

The eXp Business Model in a Nutshell

The eXp Realty business model is simple: cut the overhead, boost the value. As a fully virtual real estate brokerage, eXp eliminates the cost of brick-and-mortar offices and reinvests in technology for real estate, training, and agent support. With tools like eXp World, BoldTrail, Sisu, and full Canva Pro access, agents get everything they need to grow—without paying extra for office furniture they’ll never use.

More than 85,000 agents worldwide are thriving in this cloud-based, agent-first model—because when you remove the fluff and focus on what actually helps agents succeed, everybody wins.

What Agents Also Ask About eXp Realty Innovation

Are eXp Realty agent resources included, or do agents pay extra for them?
Core agent resources are included as part of the brokerage platform. These tools are funded through the company’s operating model rather than sold individually. Agents may still choose optional third-party services, but the primary innovation stack is built into eXp Realty support.

How often does eXp Realty release new tools or updates?
Updates occur throughout the year, with larger announcements typically made at EXPCON. Smaller enhancements and refinements are released on an ongoing basis, allowing agent systems to improve continuously rather than through infrequent major overhauls.

How does eXp Realty innovation compare to traditional brokerages?
Traditional brokerages often rely on local offices to choose tools and fund upgrades. eXp Realty centralizes innovation, allowing it to deploy consistent resources across markets without relying on franchise-level decisions or physical infrastructure investments.

Why This Matters Before You Join eXp Realty

Support through eXp Realty agent resources is designed to help agents access tools and internal infrastructure, but it does not operate independently or replace the broader eXp Realty platform.

At eXp Realty, all agents receive the same core brokerage platform, including compliance, compensation, and access to company divisions. What differs is the sponsor ecosystem an agent aligns with.

The sponsor is selected during the application process, before most agents have used the brokerage’s systems, explored its tools, or seen how sponsorship works in real life. Knowing where sponsorship fits within eXp Realty’s overall structure helps agents view this decision in the right context.

Frequently Asked Questions

Is eXp Realty innovation the same in every market?
Yes. Core agent resources and technology platforms are deployed centrally, ensuring consistency across markets. Local compliance rules vary by location, but the underlying innovation stack remains the same for all agents regardless of geography.

Does innovation replace broker or support staff at eXp Realty?
No. Technology supports efficiency, but broker teams and employee support remain responsible for compliance, approvals, and escalation. Innovation reduces friction, but human oversight and support are still central to the brokerage model.

Are agents required to use every eXp Realty tool?
No. Agents choose which resources fit their business model. Innovation expands available options but does not mandate adoption. Agents can integrate tools selectively while still operating within the eXp platform.

Can innovation slow down if market conditions change?
Because eXp Realty funds innovation through profitability rather than debt or franchise fees, investment in agent resources is designed to remain stable even during market downturns.

  • Why eXp Employees Make This the Best Brokerage for Agent Support

title: How the My eXp App Supports Agents at eXp Realty
excerpt: The My eXp app puts your real estate business in your pocket. Exp agents use it to stay productive, connected, and in control
content:
Key Takeaway: The My eXp app is eXp Realty’s centralized mobile dashboard that gives agents real-time access to production metrics, revenue share, stock information, training, and support, all in one place, without relying on multiple disconnected apps or desktop-only systems.

TL;DR About the My eXp App at eXp Realty

  • Built exclusively for eXp Realty agents
  • Centralizes sales, revenue share, and stock tracking
  • Provides direct access to training, support, and internal tools
  • Links out to eXp University. Skyslope, CRMs, Agent directory, and other resources
  • Available on both iOS and Android devices

Running a real estate business from your phone only works if the tools are actually designed for mobile use. My eXp was built to replace scattered logins, buried links, and guesswork with one centralized dashboard agents can rely on daily.

This article explains how My eXp fits into the broader eXp Realty Technology ecosystem available to eXp agents. Here’s your handy dandy index:

Table of Contents

TL;DR About the My eXp App at eXp Realty
What Is the My eXp App?
Key Features of the My eXp App
Getting Started with the My eXp App
What Agents Also Ask About the My eXp App
Why This Matters Before You Join eXp Realty
Frequently Asked Questions
Related eXp Realty Technology Guides

What Is the My eXp App?

eXp Realty is renowned for innovation and technology-driven solutions and that’s why eXp introduced the “My eXp” app. The My eXp app is basically your business command center—minus the clunky login screens and scattered browser tabs. Built exclusively for eXp Realty agents, it wraps all your most important tools into one slick, mobile-friendly package. Whether you’re checking sales, accessing training, or figuring out where that dang stock info lives, it’s all here.

Available on both iOS and Android, the My eXp dashboard is fast, intuitive, and totally built for the on-the-go reality of modern real estate. It’s not just mobile tech—it’s your mobile sidekick for winning in this business.

Key Features of the My eXp App

The My eXp app isn’t just a convenience—it’s a sanity-saver. Designed to help eXp Realty agents stay organized, productive, and in control, this mobile dashboard takes the chaos out of running your business. It’s clean, fast, and actually useful (imagine that). Here’s a look at what makes it one of the best tools in the eXp mobile arsenal:

1. Track Your Performance Metrics Without the Spreadsheet Headache

You get real-time insights into your sales, revenue share, and every key stat that actually matters. Want to know how your revenue share group is stacking up across all seven levels? It’s all right there. See your team’s FLQA status, past earnings, and even future expirations—no math degree required.

2. Your One-Stop Shop for All Things eXp (Well, Except BoldTrail)

The My eXp app links you to nearly every other eXp Realty app you need. From jumping into eXp World’s state brokers to Making It Rain, nearly everything’s just a tap away. BoldTrail CRM is the lone wolf—you’ll want to keep that one separate because you’ll be in it constantly.

3. Training That Doesn’t Require a Desktop or a Time Machine

Whether you’re new or just brushing up, you’ve got full access eXp training via eXp University, webinars, and how-to guides. It’s like having a personal coach who doesn’t ghost you. Learn how to master eXp systems, sharpen your skills, and stay ahead—anytime, anywhere.

4. Need Help? Talk to a Human (or at Least Live Chat)

Connect with the eXpert Care Desk or hop into live chat right from the app. The My eXp app makes support simple. Just tap into the Help section to connect with eXp’s eXpert Care Desk or start a live chat—available from 8 AM to 11 PM EST on weekdays, and 8 AM to 8 PM EST on weekends. More of an email-on-the-go type? There’s a handy send-email link built right in for that too.

5. Stock Check, Simplified

Your eXp Realty stock is just a click away. Open your Morgan Stanley at Work account right from the app to see what you own, what’s vested, and what that nest egg is worth today.

6. Tap Into Workplace

Join the conversation (or just lurk—we don’t judge) in eXp’s exclusive, ad-free Workplace app. It’s like Facebook, but without your aunt’s conspiracy memes and way more real estate value.

7. Instant Access to eXp Answers

The My eXp dashboard has the policies, procedures, healthcare info, and production leaderboards you usually have to dig for. Now? It’s all in one place.

8. Oh, and Everything Else Too

Want to see and buy from the eXp Swag store? Check. How about eXp Realty’s exclusive listings on Zenlist? Yup. Referral tools, buyer and seller agent toolkits, and other time-saving goodies? All here. The My eXp app is your portable HQ inside a global cloud-based real estate brokerage.

Getting Started with the My eXp App

Getting up and running with the My eXp app is ridiculously simple—because the last thing you need is one more complicated setup process. Whether you’re brand new to eXp Realty or just tired of bouncing between 47 browser tabs, this app helps you take control of your business from your phone, without needing a tech degree or a caffeine IV.

How to Download the My eXp App

No rocket science here. Just head to the Apple App Store or Google Play Store and search “My eXp”. Tap “Install” or “Get,” depending on your device, and boom—you’ve officially upgraded your mobile game. Open the app after install to make sure everything’s working like it should. And don’t skip those updates—they’re what keep your My eXp dashboard fresh and glitch-free.

Why You Should Actually Poke Around in the App

The My eXp mobile app is built exclusively for phones, which means it’s fast, sleek, and doesn’t make you pinch-zoom to find what you need. Many agents describe the app as reducing friction by centralizing access to tools they already use.

So, take a few minutes to explore. Peek into your revenue share stats, browse training options, or check how much eXp stock you’ve got tucked away. The more you use it, the more useful it becomes.

The Secret Weapon: The Menu

Want to be the agent who knows where everything lives? Spend time in the menu. From the eXp World guide and Morgan Stanley access to the Brand Store, Utility Connect, Workplace, and the ever-handy Help Center, this page is loaded with shortcuts to everything you’ll need—especially when you’re running your business between showings, closings, and texts from clients asking what escrow is (again).

Bottom line? The My eXp app is your mobile control center for all things eXp—and knowing how to navigate it means less time digging, more time doing.

What Agents Also Ask About the My eXp App

Is the My eXp app required for eXp Realty agents to use?
No. The My eXp app is optional, not mandatory. Agents can access many of the same tools through desktop portals, but the app consolidates them into a mobile-first experience. Most agents choose to use it because it simplifies access to metrics, training, and support while on the go.

How does My eXp compare to using multiple separate eXp tools?
My eXp does not replace every standalone tool, but it centralizes access to them. Instead of remembering multiple URLs and logins, agents use one dashboard that links out to training, support, stock, revenue share, and collaboration tools, reducing friction and saving time.

Who benefits most from using the My eXp app?
Agents who work remotely, travel frequently, or manage their business between appointments benefit most. New agents use it to learn where resources live, while experienced agents rely on it for quick access to metrics, support, and internal updates without sitting at a desk.

Why This Matters Before You Join eXp Realty

eXp Realty technology is designed to support agents without requiring physical offices, but it does not operate in isolation or replace the broader brokerage experience.

At eXp Realty, all agents receive the same core brokerage platform, including compliance, compensation, and access to company divisions. What differs is the sponsor ecosystem an agent aligns with.

The sponsor is selected during the application process, before most agents have used the brokerage’s systems, explored its tools, or seen how sponsorship works in real life. Knowing where sponsorship fits within eXp Realty’s overall structure helps agents view this decision in the right context.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the My eXp app used for?

The My eXp app is a mobile dashboard built for eXp Realty agents to access production metrics, revenue share, stock information, training, and internal resources. It centralizes tools that would otherwise require multiple logins, helping agents manage their business efficiently from a single mobile interface.

Is the My eXp app available on both iOS and Android devices?

Yes. The My eXp app is available for both iOS and Android devices through the Apple App Store and Google Play Store. This allows agents to access their business tools regardless of device preference, without relying on desktop-only systems or browser workarounds.

Can agents track revenue share and FLQA inside the My eXp app?

Yes. The My eXp app provides real-time visibility into revenue share earnings, FLQA status, and downline metrics. Agents can monitor progress across all seven levels, review historical earnings, and see upcoming expirations without needing spreadsheets or separate reporting tools.

Does the My eXp app include training and education resources?

Yes. The app includes direct access to eXp University, live and recorded training sessions, and educational resources. This allows agents to participate in ongoing education, onboarding support, and skill development without needing to log in from a desktop computer.

Can agents contact support through the My eXp app?

Yes. Agents can access eXp’s eXpert Care Desk and live chat support directly from the My eXp app. Email support options are also available, making it easier to get answers quickly without searching for separate contact channels.

title: Your Ultimate Guide to eXp Realty Teams: Structure, Splits, and Team Perks
excerpt: Key Points eXp Realty teams come in four structures: Domestic, Self-Organized, Standard, and Mega Icon Sponsor teams and organized teams are different—and yes, you can…
content:
Key Points

  • eXp Realty teams come in four structures: Domestic, Self-Organized, Standard, and Mega Icon
  • Sponsor teams and organized teams are different—and yes, you can be part of both
  • Team leaders and members split the commission, but each also pays eXp individually
  • Only full-cap team members can qualify for ICON status
  • You can add team members in other states with proper compliance steps
  • Domestic teams share one cap; Standard and Mega Icon teams offer reduced caps
  • Transaction fees are per deal, not per person, and they’re shared

So, You Wanna Start or Join an eXp Realty Team? Buckle Up.eXp Realty teams aren’t just buzzwords and matching headshots—they’re high-functioning groups of real estate teammates built for support, growth, and smart commission splits. Whether you’re leading a crew or just trying to figure out how real estate teams work, you’re in the right place.

Teams at eXp Realty come in different flavors—Domestic, Standard, Mega Icon, Self-Organized—and each offers its own perks, structure, and split strategy. Powered by cloud-based tools and a global brokerage backbone, the eXp team structure helps agents scale faster without all the office drama.

In this guide, we’ll cover how eXp teams are built and how the money flows. Whether you’re hunting for the top eXp Realty teams near you or trying to name your own squad (please don’t choose “First Team Real Estate”), we’ve got answers.

Let’s break down how the best real estate team structures work—and what kind of team value you should expect.

Table of Contents

The Two Types of eXp Realty Teams You Need to Know About
What Is a Real Estate Team, Really?
Exploring eXp Realty Team Structures: Which One Fits Your Business?
How Do eXp Realty Commission Splits Work on a Team?
Can Team Members ICON at eXp Realty?
Can You Add Team Members in Another State?
How eXp Realty Collaboration and Smart Agent Alliance Can Help You Grow
Frequently Asked Questions

The Two Types of eXp Realty Teams You Need to Know About

Before we get into the nitty gritty of commission splits and eXp team structures, let’s make one thing clear: saying you’re on an eXp Realty team can mean two very different things. Let’s break down the two types of teams you’ll hear about at eXp.

Sponsor Teams: Bonus Support (If You Pick the Right One)

Sponsor teams—like Smart Agent Alliance—are made up of agents who offer tools, mentorship, community, and training to their downline. When you name a Smart Agent Alliance founder as your eXp sponsor, you’re plugging into proven systems that would normally cost thousands (and sometimes still underdeliver). The value? All included—no extra charge.

But here’s the catch: most eXp sponsors aren’t part of a structured sponsor team. And they’re not required to offer you anything beyond their name for your join eXp application. So when you hear “meet our team” or see someone pushing an eXp sponsor team, make sure they’re actually offering value and not just a Canva logo and vibes.

Organized Teams: Where Structure Meets Splits

This blog? It’s about the other kind—organized real estate teams at eXp. These are formal business setups with a team leader, one or more real estate teammates, and a signed agreement that lays out how the whole thing runs. Think of it like a mini brokerage within the brokerage.

You get defined commission splits, assigned roles, and a system built to make the business more scalable, without the overhead of a traditional office. eXp stays hands-off when it comes to internal team drama, so make sure your contracts are airtight and your expectations are crystal clear.

Can You Join a Sponsor Team and an Organized Team at eXp?

And yes, you can absolutely be part of a sponsor team and also join an organized team. They serve two totally different purposes, and finding both your ideal sponsor and a great organized team can give you the best of both worlds: support, mentorship, structure, and scalability.

Now that we’ve sorted that out, let’s talk about what real estate teams actually are—and how they work.

What Is a Real Estate Team, Really?

At its core, a real estate team is a group of agents and support staff working under a structured system to move deals faster and serve clients better. These teams are like well-oiled machines—everyone’s got a job, and no one’s stuck doing five roles badly.

You’ll often find buyer specialists who focus solely on guiding clients through the purchase process while listing agents handle pricing strategy, sometimes using tools like Curbio to get properties market-ready without upfront costs.  Transaction coordinators keep the paperwork flowing with help from Skyslopee, and admin pros handle everything from scheduling to CRM upkeep. This specialization helps every team member operate in their zone of genius, and helps clients feel like they’re getting VIP treatment at every stage.

But not every real estate agent team runs like a corporate org chart. Some real estate teams operate more casually: they generate leads together, share marketing resources, and cheer each other on, while each agent still runs their own deals from start to finish.

No matter the style, the goal’s the same: streamline the chaos, deliver top-notch service, and grow faster than you could solo.

Exploring eXp Realty Team Structures: Which One Fits Your Business?

So, you’ve got your eye on the eXp Realty team model—but which version? Just like real estate clients, not all teams come with the same needs, paperwork, or personalities. Luckily, eXp Realty gives you options.

There are four main eXp team structures, and each comes with its own rules around production requirements, commission splits, and team size. Whether you’re rolling solo with your spouse or scaling to empire status, eXp has a setup for you.

Let’s break it down—without the boring parts.

1. Domestic Teams: Just the Two of You (and One Cap)

Domestic Teams are exactly what they sound like—a two-agent power couple setup. You and your spouse (or domestic partner) team up, share one $16K cap, and work your market like the duo you are. All you need is proof you file taxes together or a marriage certificate.

This real estate team structure is ideal for agents who share more than just a CRM. You’re working together day in and day out, making joint decisions, splitting the load, and probably negotiating who’s doing dinner between showings. Efficiency and flexibility with built-in support—plus, no need for complex team paperwork.

2. Self-Organized Teams: Casual Vibes, Solid Collaboration

A Self-Organized Team at eXp is basically the “we’re all friends who hustle together” model. There are no official cap reduction or team lead requirements here—just agents working together in a loosely defined structure. You can share leads, systems, and memes. You’re independent but cooperative.

There’s also no limit to how big your self-organized team can get, and as long as there’s no formal team agreement, you can even have multiple side teams or informal alliances. This structure is perfect for entrepreneurial agents who want teamwork without a hierarchy.

3. Standard Teams: The Startup Squad with Structure

Want reduced caps for your real estate teammates and a bit more structure? Time to talk Standard Teams.

To qualify, your team leader must have closed 30 transactions or $6 million in volume over the last 12 months. Team members can choose a reduced $8K cap, but that comes with a catch: the team lead must receive at least 25% of each transaction.

This setup creates clear roles, workflows, and revenue-sharing that help newer agents get mentorship while more experienced ones scale faster. It’s a smart option for those looking to run a lean, productive, and profitable eXp Realty team—without needing $40M in production (yet) and while still keeping more commission than with so-called 100% commission brokerages.

4. Mega Icon Teams: For the Agents Who Run Things

Mega Icon Teams are the top-tier model at eXp. These teams are for leaders who’ve closed $40 million or 175 transactions in the last 12 months, and have at least 10 team members who would’ve capped individually. Some may tap into resources like eXp Commercial too.

The benefits? Huge. Team members can opt into a $4K cap (yes, really), but they’ll need to give the team lead that same minimum of 25% per transaction. There’s no cap on how large these teams can grow, and because they’re producing at volume, they often have access to more tools, higher-level training, and massive scalability potential.

Think of Mega Icon Teams as the top eXp Realty teams—elite squads designed for domination, expansion, and long-term growth through revenue share.

A Quick Comparison of eXp Realty Team Types

How Do eXp Realty Commission Splits Work on a Team?

Let’s talk about everyone’s favorite topic: getting paid. Specifically, how much of it you actually keep when you’re on an eXp Realty team—because if you think your team leader takes a cut and that’s the end of it… Surprise! eXp wants their piece too.

Here’s the deal: being part of an organized real estate team often means you’re splitting commission twice—once with your team leader, and then again with eXp. It’s not a scam—it’s just how commission splits for teams are structured. Knowing how the dollars flow can save you from some serious math shock on payday.

How Commissions Are Calculated on eXp Teams

Here’s the general order of operations:

  1. Any outside referrals owed to other brokers come off the top.
  2. The remaining gross commission is split between the team leader and team member based on your agreement (commonly 50/50).
  3. Then, eXp’s company dollar (the 20% split up to your cap) and transaction fees are deducted from each agent’s share individually.
  4. Fees like the broker review fee, capped transaction fee, and risk management fee are per transaction—not per agent—so they’re usually split 50/50.

Real Example: $10,000 Gross Commission Deal

Assumptions:

  • 50/50 split between the team leader and the team member
  • Team leader is capped
  • Team member is not capped

Let’s say your team just closed a deal for $10,000. High five.
Now the fun begins…

  • $5,000 goes to the Team Leader
  • $5,000 goes to the Team Member

Easy, right? Not so fast.

Team Leader’s Cut (Capped):

  • Start with $5,000
  • Subtract 50% of capped transaction fee ($250) ? $5,000 – $125 = $4,875
  • Subtract 50% of broker review fee ($25) ? $4,875 – $12.50 = $4,862.50
  • Subtract 50% of risk management fee ($60) ? $4,862.50 – $30 = $4,832.50 net

Team Member’s Cut (Uncapped):

  • Start with $5,000
  • Subtract 20% to eXp ($1,000) ? $4,000
  • Subtract 50% of broker review fee ($12.50) ? $3,987.50
  • Subtract 50% of the risk management fee ($30) ? $3,957.50 net

Wait—So I’m Paying Both?

Yep. When you’re on an eXp Realty team, your team leader gets their agreed-upon cut (at least 25% if you’re on a reduced cap like in Standard or Mega Icon Teams), and eXp still collects their usual 20% company dollar and transaction fees from you directly.

So even if you’re handing 25% of your commission to your team lead, you’re still on the hook for your personal split with eXp.

And all those little fees? Broker review, risk management, capped transaction fees—they get split between you and the team leader per transaction, not per team. So don’t be surprised when you both see those line items pop up on your statement. Welcome to the wild world of real estate team structures. It’s not complicated once you see how it flows, but it is important to understand before your first payday surprise.

Can Team Members ICON at eXp Realty?

Ah, the ICON Agent ICON Agent Award—a shiny badge of honor and a nice chunk of your cap back in stock. But if you’re part of an eXp Realty team, especially an organized one, you’re probably wondering: Do team members still qualify for ICON?

Short answer: Yes, but it depends on your cap.

Here’s how it works across the different eXp team structures:

  • Domestic Teams: Only one ICON award is available per team since you’re sharing one $16K cap. So if you’re in a two-person Domestic Team, you’ll have to fight it out (or, you know, just agree on it like adults).
  • Self-Organized Teams: Every agent is on a full $16K cap here, which means every team member can qualify for ICON independently. No restrictions. Just hustle close deals, and get rewarded.
  • Standard Teams: Team members can still ICON—but only if they opt to stay on the full $16K cap instead of taking the reduced $8K cap option. If they go with the lower cap, they won’t be eligible. So it’s basically a choice: save money upfront or play the long game for ICON benefits.
  • Mega Icon Teams: Same rules as Standard Teams, just scaled up. Members can ICON if they stay on the full $16K cap. If they choose the reduced $4K cap, ICON’s off the table.

So yes, eXp Realty team members can achieve ICON status—but only if they’re willing to commit to the full cap. If you’re running the numbers, just know that ICON status comes with some serious perks, including up to $16,000 in stock incentives. Worth considering if you’re aiming high.

Can You Add Team Members in Another State?

So your eXp Realty team is up and running, and now you’re thinking bigger—like multi-state bigger. Maybe you’ve got a rockstar agent in Texas, but your team’s based in California. Can they join your team?

Yes, team members in another state can be added to your organized eXp Realty team—but don’t skip over the fine print.

Here’s the deal: crossing state lines means crossing into a jungle of licensing laws, broker supervision rules, and set-up logistics. Every state has its real estate regulatory circus, and eXp needs to make sure your team is compliant no matter where it operates.

Before adding that out-of-state agent, you’ll need to loop in teamservices@exprealty.net or hop into the Onboarding & Agent Support Lobby. They’ll walk you through how to handle the different state requirements, paperwork, and onboarding steps.

One exception? Domestic Teams. If you’re in a Domestic Team and want to add another state, both partners must be licensed in that same state. Otherwise, it won’t fly—and commissions can’t be processed without referrals.

So yes, you can build a cross-state real estate team at eXp. Just make sure you’re not winging it, because “winging it” doesn’t go over well with state real estate commissions.

Frequently Asked Questions

Can team members on eXp Realty teams qualify for ICON?

Yes, but only if they choose to stay on the full $16,000 cap. Reduced-cap team members (like those on Standard or Mega Icon Teams) are not eligible for ICON status. Domestic teams can share the cap but only one can earn ICON.

What is the difference between a sponsor team and an organized team at eXp?

Sponsor teams offer optional coaching, tools, and community support to agents in their downline. Organized teams are formal business entities with commission splits, agreements, and roles under eXp’s official team structure.

Can I be in both a sponsor team and an organized team?

Yes. Sponsor teams and organized teams serve different purposes. You can be in both at the same time and benefit from the support and structure each offers.

How are commissions split on eXp Realty teams?

The commission is first split between the team leader and team member (often 25 – 50% going to the team leader). Then, eXp deducts its 20% company split (unless the agent has capped) and applicable transaction fees from each agent’s share individually.

Are transaction fees split or charged per person?

Fees like the broker review and risk management fee are charged per transaction, not per agent. That means they are typically split between team members.

Can eXp Realty teams include agents from different states?

Yes, but compliance rules vary by state. You’ll need to coordinate with eXp’s Team Services to ensure all legal requirements are met. Domestic team members must both be licensed in the same state.

title: How Making It Rain Leads Work at eXp Realty
excerpt: Making It Rain at eXp Realty runs PPC ads for you, sending buyer and seller leads into your CRM. Smart Agent Alliance shows how to make it pay off.
content:
Key Takeaway: Making It Rain at eXp Realty is a brokerage-operated PPC advertising program that runs Google and Meta ads on behalf of agents and delivers buyer and seller leads directly into the agent’s CRM. It matters most to agents who want paid online leads without personally managing ad platforms, vendors, or campaign setup.

TL;DR About Making It Rain at eXp Realty

  • Making It Rain is eXp Realty’s internal PPC advertising program
  • Campaigns are managed by eXp’s in-house marketing team
  • Ads run on Google and Meta platforms under the agent’s branding
  • Leads are delivered directly into the agent’s connected CRM
  • No referral fee is charged at closing for these leads
  • Participation is optional and billed monthly
  • The program sits inside eXp’s broader Revenos lead ecosystem

Many agents want online buyer and seller leads but do not want to become part-time digital advertisers. Managing Google PPC, Meta ads, targeting rules, compliance, landing pages, and tracking requires time, testing, and ongoing adjustment. Most agents either outsource this work to third-party vendors or stop altogether after inconsistent results.

Making It Rain exists to remove that operational burden by centralizing paid advertising inside the brokerage.

This article explains how Making It Rain at eXp Realty fits into the broader eXp Realty leads ecosystem available to eXp agents. Here’s your handy dandy index:

Table of Contents

Which Brokerages Offer Lead Generation And Why Making It Rain Stands Out
Making It Rain Lead Types: Home Search, Express Offers, FlexReach
How Making It Rain Connects To Your CRM And Tech For Auto Leads
What Does Making It Rain Cost?
Are Making It Rain Leads Any Good? Follow Up, Quality, And Results
What Agents Also Ask About Making It Rain
Why This Matters Before You Join eXp Realty
Frequently Asked Questions
Related eXp Realty Lead Tools

How Does Making It Rain Work?

Most agents who search for “Which brokerages offer lead generation services for real estate agents” are not looking for slogans. They want a real system. Making It Rain eXp Realty is that system inside eXp. It was created by Glenn Sanford when the company started and now sits inside eXp Revenos, eXp’s full-time lead generation division. In simple terms, Making It Rain eXp Realty is the in house real estate PPC leads program that sits under eXp Revenos and powers Google PPC real estate leads and Facebook ads for real estate agents, all tied directly into eXp’s CRM choices.

You begin by picking your campaign type, budget, and target area. Inside the Making It Rain dashboard you choose Home Search Leads, Express Offers leads, or FlexReach, then set your ad spend. eXp’s internal team handles the tech, targeting, and tracking.

Mary H. talks about eXp’s model as “built for agents,” because the training, collaboration, and systems all work together. She says a big part of her consistency comes from leaning into eXp’s offerings instead of trying to build everything alone. The relationships and tools gave her a more steady, predictable business base.

In plain English, Making It Rain is like hiring a full-time ad team and paying them a gym membership fee instead of a full salary.

Which Brokerages Offer Lead Generation And Why Making It Rain Stands Out

Many brokerages say they “provide leads,” but most rely on third party portals, team leaders, or office floor time. eXp Realty is different. It built a full-time internal lead division, Revenos, and programs like Making It Rain and Zoocasa.

Agents search things like “Best brokerages for agents looking to get buyer and seller leads” or “Brokerages offering paid lead packages for real estate agents” because they feel stuck between expensive portals and broker promises. Traditional firms often depend on Zillow, Realtor.com, or office leads that appear and disappear with the market.

At eXp, Revenos and Making It Rain are built into the model. Instead of waiting for your broker to hand you a lead, you log in, choose your campaign, and let eXp’s marketing team run the ads under your name and branding. You are not fighting over a shared pool. You are launching your own pipeline.

This also answers a quiet question: “How do brokerages provide leads to their affiliated agents?” In many places, it is through random rotation or whoever is in the office. With Making It Rain, the agent who orders the campaign gets the leads. No games, no silent priority list.

Artemisa B. came to the U.S. from Mexico and started from zero in a new market. Over time, by leaning into the systems and support at eXp, she closed 223 deals as a solo agent. She credits the company’s tools and leverage for letting her grow far beyond what her old brokerage model allowed.

If the average “lead program” is a leaky bucket, Making It Rain is closer to a faucet you can actually turn on and off without breaking the handle.

Making It Rain Lead Types: Home Search, Express Offers, FlexReach

Making It Rain offers three main campaign types. Home Search Leads capture buyers searching Google for homes in your area. Express Offers Leads attract motivated sellers who want cash or quick options. FlexReach is a flexible product that promotes events, retargets your database, or reaches custom audiences using uploaded lists or geotargeting.

When agents ask “Brokerages that supply exclusive leads to their agents?” what they really mean is “Can I choose the type of business I want?” Making It Rain is structured around that idea. Each campaign type serves a clear purpose and uses channels that match how buyers and sellers actually behave online.

Home Search Leads

These campaigns focus on buyers searching phrases like “homes for sale in Austin” or “three bedroom house in Novato.” eXp’s team runs Google PPC ads that send people to your IDX home search site. They register to see more listings. The lead lands in your CRM with search behavior attached for smarter follow up.

Express Offers Leads

Express Offers leads are seller focused. These campaigns speak to owners who are open to cash offers, convenience, or speed. The ads often run on Facebook and drive people to a simple form. These are highly motivated sellers, but they still need options, education, and a skilled agent to help them choose the best path.

FlexReach

FlexReach is the flexible tool. You can upload a list of past clients, event registrants, or cold leads, or you can geotarget a neighborhood. eXp runs ads across channels like Google and Meta, and in some campaigns even Amazon, to stay in front of that specific audience. It is perfect for events, open houses, webinars, or “Is now a good time to sell?” reminders.

Together, Home Search Leads, Express Offers leads, and FlexReach form the core of the Making It Rain leads system at eXp Realty, giving agents a clear mix of buyer, seller, and custom campaign options inside one program.

Agent Joel B. used FlexReach to promote an “Agents Helping Agents” event in Buffalo. He uploaded a list of local agents and let the system target them across multiple platforms. The people he wanted to reach saw the event everywhere. Ticket sales jumped, and the audience showed up already warmed up by the marketing.

FlexReach is basically the Swiss Army knife of Making It Rain, only you will actually use this tool more than once.

How Making It Rain Connects To Your CRM And Tech For Auto Leads

Making It Rain leads do not sit in your email. They are pushed straight into your CRM, such as Lofty, or BoldTrail. The MIR dashboard shows clicks, leads, and performance. You adjust locations, budgets, and campaign types in one place, then let your automations handle speed to lead and follow up.

Agents who search “Brokerages with technology platforms that generate leads automatically?” or “Brokerages that integrate CRM systems for lead management?” are looking for exactly this setup. At eXp, Making It Rain runs the ads. Your CRM receives the contacts. You build automation rules once, then let the system notify you like a digital assistant that never sleeps.

Once a Home Search or Express Offers lead registers, your CRM can send a text, email, and task in seconds. You can also tag FlexReach leads differently, such as past clients or “likely seller,” so the right scripts and follow up plans fire.

Inside the MIR dashboard, you see what is happening. You can filter by campaign, see cost per lead, and stop or change campaigns without opening three different ad platforms. For agents who are still juggling spreadsheets and sticky notes, this alone feels like air.

ICON agent Raj J. explains that fifty to sixty percent of his business comes from a mix of sphere referrals and online leads through tools like Boldtrail landing pages. He says eXp’s systems helped him diversify, automate, and keep scaling even when others slowed down, because the tech kept feeding his pipeline.

If your current “CRM integration” is just copying phone numbers from your inbox, Making It Rain will feel like moving from a flip phone to a smart phone overnight.

What Does Making It Rain Cost?

Making It Rain is a paid monthly subscription that starts around $250 per month. You do not pay referral fees at closing. Membership is month to month, with flexible budgets. You close deals on your normal eXp split, then decide whether to scale, hold steady, or pause your campaigns.

Many agents type “Brokerages with subscription services for lead access?” or “What are the commission structures for brokerages that provide agent leads?” because they have already felt the sting of a 50 or 60 percent split on “team leads.” Making It Rain flips that model. You pay for marketing, not for a permanent haircut on every check.

Here is the simple math. If you spend $250 to $500 per month and close even one reasonable transaction every few months, the ROI usually beats most paid lead programs that take a large cut at closing. You still keep your standard eXp commission split. There is no extra company split just because the lead came from Making It Rain. Independent real estate lead generation statistics for agents show many agents spend a few hundred dollars per month on paid leads, often with less structure than Making It Rain.

You can also scale up or down without asking permission. Have a busy season coming and want more buyer leads in a specific city or zip code. Increase the budget. Need to slow down during a life event or deep focus period. Pause or lower it. That flexibility is hard to find in traditional “lead packages.”

Agent Jill R. started with one accidental sale and treated real estate as a side project. After plugging into eXp’s systems, training, and lead tools, she went on to close 147 homes without holding listings. She credits the model and support for turning a small one off win into a full-time business.

Compared to handing over half your commission forever, a subscription you can turn off is the financial version of choosing a rental car instead of buying the whole dealership.

Are Making It Rain Leads Any Good? Follow Up, Quality, And Results

Making It Rain leads are as good as your follow up. Express Offers campaigns often attract very motivated sellers, while Home Search and FlexReach leads build a broad pipeline. The real edge comes from speed, empathy, and persistence. Most agents under call. The ones who win treat every lead as a conversation, not a quick sale.

When agents ask “How do brokerages vet and distribute leads to their agents?” they usually want to know if they are getting leftovers. With Making It Rain, you are not in a round robin. You receive leads from the campaigns you ordered. The key question becomes “What do you do in the first five minutes and the next thirty days?”

NAR data shows the average agent calls a lead about half the time it needs. Real contact often takes eight to twelve attempts across calls, texts, and emails. With Express Offers, these are often sellers under stress. Opening with “Tell me your story, what is going on?” builds trust. Sometimes the best fit is a retail sale, not a cash offer, yet that honest talk often wins the listing.

Armando G., a sponsor team member, built a system that closes more than twenty deals per month from Facebook ads. His story is simple. The ad gets the click, but the magic is in the conversation that follows. Consistent scripts and follow up turned paid leads into a predictable business.

If your current lead plan is “call once, blame the lead,” Making It Rain will work about as well as a treadmill used as a clothes rack. The power is in actually climbing on and pressing start.

What Agents Also Ask About Making It Rain

Is Making It Rain required to succeed at eXp Realty?
No. Making It Rain is optional. Many agents at eXp Realty build successful businesses using referrals, sphere, and organic lead sources. Making It Rain exists for agents who want paid online lead generation without managing ad platforms themselves, not as a requirement for production or advancement.

Who is Making It Rain best suited for?
Making It Rain works best for agents who already have a functioning CRM, automation, and consistent follow-up habits. Paid leads require fast response and structured nurture. Agents without established systems may struggle to convert any PPC leads effectively, regardless of the source.

How does Making It Rain compare to third-party PPC vendors?
Making It Rain centralizes PPC campaign management inside eXp Realty instead of outsourcing to outside vendors. Agents still fund advertising spend, but campaign setup, compliance, optimization, and CRM integration are handled internally, reducing complexity and minimizing the risk of policy or branding violations.

Does Making It Rain replace other eXp Realty lead programs?
No. Making It Rain supplements other eXp Realty lead programs such as Revenos and Zoocasa. Each program serves a different function within the overall lead ecosystem, allowing agents to layer paid advertising alongside referral-based or structured lead sources.

Why This Matters Before You Join eXp Realty

Programs like Making It Rain are designed to support agent-funded lead generation through structured campaigns, but they do not operate in isolation or replace the broader eXp Realty experience.

At eXp Realty, all agents receive the same core brokerage platform, including compliance, compensation, and access to company divisions. What differs is the sponsor ecosystem an agent aligns with.

The sponsor is selected during the application process, before most agents have used the brokerage’s systems, explored its tools, or seen how sponsorship works in real life. Knowing where sponsorship fits within eXp Realty’s overall structure helps agents view this decision in the right context.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is Making It Rain at eXp Realty?
Making It Rain is eXp Realty’s internal paid advertising program that allows agents to generate buyer and seller leads through brokerage-managed PPC campaigns. eXp oversees campaign setup, compliance, and delivery, while leads are routed directly into the agent’s CRM for follow-up and conversion.

How does pricing work for Making It Rain?
Making It Rain operates on a monthly subscription model in addition to the agent’s advertising spend. Agents are not charged referral fees at closing for these leads. Pricing structures, minimum budgets, and campaign options may vary and are subject to change based on program parameters.

Do Making It Rain leads affect commission splits or capping?
No. Transactions generated through Making It Rain close under the agent’s standard eXp commission split and count toward the annual cap in the same way as self-generated business. There are no additional referral deductions tied to these leads beyond normal brokerage terms.

Can Making It Rain campaigns be adjusted or paused?
Yes. Agents can generally adjust budgets, targeting locations, and campaign focus through the Making It Rain platform, subject to program guidelines and availability. Flexibility allows agents to scale spending up or down based on capacity, market conditions, or changes in business priorities.

Is Making It Rain available to new agents?
New agents may access Making It Rain after licensing and onboarding at eXp Realty. However, conversion success depends heavily on having a functioning CRM, prompt follow-up habits, and basic lead conversion training. Without systems in place, paid leads may not perform effectively.

Related eXp Realty Lead Tools

  • Zoocasa: Hot Leads Only Available at eXp Realty
  • Should Realtors Rent Leads or Own Their Pipeline?
  • Lead Generation Got Smarter: Meet eXp’s “My Link My Lead” Tool

You also get a massive stockpile of fonts, stock images, animations, and access to Canva real estate templates designed specifically for agents. Translation? You can finally ditch the generic clipart and create scroll-stopping content that won’t get lost in a feed full of filters and cat videos.

Postcards? Check. Property websites? Yup. Direct mail pieces, infographics, listing presentations, and everything in between? Absolutely. Canva lets you make it all—fast. And with the eXp Listings App, you can drag and drop property info and photos straight into your designs without breaking a sweat.

The background remover is perfect for cleaning up cluttered photos, and the resize tool helps your post go from Instagram square to email header in one click.

Once you’ve built your masterpiece, download it in whatever format works best—PDF, PNG, you name it. Want to share it? Post directly to social or send it to your team. Either way, you’ll pass for having a full-time designer… even if your “team” is just you and a latte.

Advantages of the New System

Moving to eXp Canva comes with a lot of benefits that easily elevate your campaigns. More importantly, it equips agents with effective tools and smart workflows designed for success. By integrating Canva Pro free tools, eXp Realty makes marketing more accessible, more efficient, and way less stressful—giving agents a professional edge without adding another line item to the budget.

Enhanced Customization Options for Agents

Canva gives you serious flexibility when it comes to design. Agents can fine-tune templates to match their personal branding, while pulling from Canva’s deep well of icons, graphics, and illustrations.

With features like Magic Resize, you can instantly reformat your content for multiple channels—perfect when you want to go from a postcard to an Instagram Story without starting from scratch.

Streamlined Marketing Material Creation Process

The workflow inside eXp Canva is built for on-the-go agents. With its click-and-edit setup, agents can quickly produce social content, brochures, and more—without feeling like they’re stuck in a tech tutorial.

Prebuilt templates, smart suggestions, and real-time collaboration mean you can jump in, get it done, and move on to your next deal.

And the Canva Content Planner? It’s a total time-saver, letting you schedule social posts directly from the platform. No tab overload. No juggling apps.

Professional Branding Made Accessible

Branding doesn’t have to be a headache. With eXp Canva’s Brand Kit, agents can store their logos, fonts, and color palettes in one place, making it ridiculously easy to keep everything consistent and polished.

Premium visuals and high-quality real estate Canva templates make your marketing pop—whether it’s a listing flyer or a social campaign. It all works seamlessly with eXp marketing guidelines, so you can stay compliant without even thinking about it.

Broader Impacts on eXp Agents

Rolling out Canva Enterprise across the eXp ecosystem wasn’t just a software decision—it was a strategic shift. As more virtual real estate brokerages lean into tech-forward models, this move helps eXp agents do more, faster, with better results.

Because when your marketing looks sharp and takes minutes, not hours? You’re not just keeping up—you’re closing more deals.

Less Time Designing, More Time Closing

Outdated tools are out. Smart, nimble platforms are in. With customizable templates and smart features like AI-powered writing tools, agents are cutting content creation time—and seeing it pay off in real numbers.

Tech That Actually Moves the Needle

This isn’t about looking trendy. It’s about tech that works. With automation, built-in efficiency, and seamless integration into the eXp Realty ecosystem, this platform helps agents market like modern pros—no coding required.

Q4. Give agents the right tools, and they don’t just adapt—they outperform.

What Agents Also Ask About Canva Pro at eXp Realty

Is Canva Pro really free for all eXp Realty agents?

Yes. Canva Pro access is included for all active eXp Realty agents as part of the brokerage technology platform. Agents do not pay a separate subscription fee for Canva Pro features when using eXp’s marketing center.

How does eXp Canva differ from a standard Canva account?

Who benefits most from Canva Pro at eXp?

Yes. All active eXp Realty agents receive Canva Pro access through the eXp Marketing Center at no additional cost. This includes premium design features, real estate templates, stock images, and brand tools that would otherwise require a paid Canva subscription outside the brokerage.

For many agents, Canva Pro can replace the need for a designer for everyday marketing. Listing flyers, social posts, presentations, and mailers can be created in-house. Some agents may still hire designers for specialty branding projects, but routine marketing can typically be handled independently.

No. Canva is built specifically for non-designers. Its drag-and-drop interface, pre-sized templates, and guided layouts make it easy for agents to create professional-looking materials quickly, even without prior design experience or technical skills.

Yes. Templates available inside eXp Canva are created to align with eXp Realty branding guidelines. This helps reduce the risk of off-brand marketing while still allowing agents flexibility to personalize designs for their market, listings, and individual business identity.

title: Final Walk Through Before Closing: What Agents Need to Know
excerpt: Key Points Final walk through confirms the home’s condition before closing Check that all agreed repairs are complete Ensure the seller removed all personal items…
content:
Key Points

  • Final walk through confirms the home’s condition before closing
  • Check that all agreed repairs are complete
  • Ensure the seller removed all personal items
  • Make sure the condition hasn’t changed
  • Document any damage or last-minute issues
  • Final walk through generally happens 24–48 hours before close
  • Skipping it risks costly surprises after closing

Ah, the final walk through before closing—where you get one more shot to discover the seller’s idea of “move-in ready” means “oops, we took the fridge and the closet doors.” Think it’s just a formality? Around 1 in 20 final walk throughs uncover issues serious enough to kill the deal.

Whether it’s a light fixture swap-out that wasn’t in the deal or a surprise hole in the drywall, this isn’t the time to play it cool. A pre-closing walkthrough (or walk-through, or walkthrough—pick your preferred punctuation) is where buyers confirm that everything is still standing, functional, and as promised

So yeah, skipping it? Not recommended unless you enjoy filing complaints after you’ve already unpacked the moving truck. From who attends the final walk through to what to look for during it, we’re covering everything to help you nail this last step like the real estate pro you are. Here’s your handy dandy index:

Table of Contents

What Is a Final Walk Through Before Closing?
Steps If You Find Issues During the Walkthrough

What Is a Final Walk Through Before Closing?

The final walk-through isn’t just a feel-good victory lap around the house. It’s your buyer’s last shot to make sure they’re not inheriting a leaky ceiling, a mysteriously absent stove, or “repairs” done with duct tape and hope. This is the moment to confirm that the property condition still matches the terms in the purchase agreement.

The goal? A clean and accurate property condition report, ideally without discovering a missing garage door or surprise pet damage. And if the home’s condition doesn’t match the contract, the buyer can hit the brakes faster than a bad credit score at underwriting.

Why the Final Walkthrough Actually Matters

This final check isn’t a chance to redecorate or get second thoughts on the paint color. It’s to confirm that repairs were done, nothing new is broken, and the seller didn’t swap your included appliances for something from 1992.

This is your buyer’s last shot at a property condition assessment before the keys change hands. If that fridge is gone or the AC stopped working—now’s the time to catch it. Not after closing, when it becomes your buyer’s expensive surprise.

When Does the Walkthrough Happen and Who Shows Up?

So when is the final walk through? Usually 24 to 48 hours before closing—right when everyone’s nerves are already fried. That gives just enough time to flag issues and maybe negotiate before the documents start flying.

Who attends the walkthrough? At a minimum the buyer and their real estate agent. Sometimes the seller tags along (usually awkwardly), and if the home’s older or had major repairs, bringing a home inspector can offer some extra peace of mind.

Depending on the size of the home, expect this step to take anywhere from a quick 15-minute breeze-through to a full hour-long real estate walkthrough. Pro tip: doing this in an empty house is much easier. You’ll spot damage, scuffs, and last-minute “oops” moments the seller left behind.

Setting the Right Expectations for the Buyer

Before stepping foot into the walkthrough, your buyer needs to know exactly what’s going down—and what’s definitely not. This isn’t the time to start spotting things they missed during the inspection and suddenly expect a new roof. The final walk through is a confirmation mission, not a second inspection with bonus drama.

Final Walkthrough vs. Home Inspection: Know the Difference

Let’s clear up the classic confusion: a home inspection happens early in the deal. That’s when buyers get the full diagnostic on the home—roof, foundation, outlets, ghost infestations, the works. Any problems found there? That’s when you negotiate repairs or concessions.

The final walk through, on the other hand, happens near the finish line. Think of it like a pre-delivery check. You’re there to make sure nothing’s changed since the deal was struck. It’s a verification step, not a redo of the inspection.

What the Final Walkthrough Is For:

Let’s keep it simple. The walkthrough before closing is for:
• Confirming agreed-upon repairs are actually done (no, sticky notes that say “FIX ME” don’t count)
• Ensuring the seller’s stuff is gone—not “mostly packed”
• Verifying everything that worked before still works now

What the Final Walkthrough Is Not For:

This is not your buyer’s chance to:
• Suddenly demand a full remodel
• Reopen negotiations because the wall color “hits different in daylight”
• Delay closing over stuff that should’ve been caught weeks ago

Make sure your buyer knows the rules of the final walkthrough checklist game. Because nothing kills a vibe—or a sale—like unrealistic expectations and last-minute finger-pointing. Clear expectations = smooth closing. You’re welcome.

Essential Final Walk Through Checklist (a.k.a. The “Let’s Not Regret This” List)

The final walk through before closing isn’t the time to wing it with good vibes and crossed fingers. Bring a real checklist and treat this like what it is—a last opportunity to make sure you’re not about to inherit someone else’s half-finished honey-do list. Snap pics of anything sketchy to back up your concerns and protect your buyer (and your commission).

Here’s what to check during your final walkthrough real estate adventure:

1. Verify Inspection Repairs

Remember all those inspection-related fixes the seller promised? Time to confirm they actually got done. Plumbing, HVAC, windows, stains from unknown origins—if it was flagged, it needs to be handled. Ask for receipts or contractor invoices. No proof = no peace of mind.

2. Ensure Personal Items Are Gone

Unless the contract says you’re inheriting the seller’s treadmill or ancient cat tree, the house should be empty. “Broom clean” does not mean “bonus boxes in the attic.” This step is part of the verification of property condition—your buyer should walk into a clean slate, not a leftover yard sale.

3. Examine the Interior – Any New Problems?

Do a full sweep for anything that wasn’t broken, dented, or mysteriously leaking the last time you saw the place. Sometimes the movers play fast and loose with doorways—and drywall. Check for:

  • New holes or scuffs
  • Damaged hardware
  • Doors or garage systems that no longer function

The goal of this final pre-closing check is to ensure the home didn’t take a beating on its way out the door. If something looks off, flag it now—because after closing, that repair bill becomes the buyer’s parting gift.

4. Examine Outdoor Areas

Don’t forget to walk the exterior. The final walk through of the house includes the backyard too. Fences suddenly falling? Moved staging pot uncovered a big hole in the deck? No one wants to discover a broken sprinkler system on move-in day—or worse, a pile of debris that didn’t make it into the moving truck.

Steps If You Find Issues During the Walkthrough

So, you’re 24 hours from closing, and the final check just revealed a fridge gone rogue or a couch-sized dent in the drywall. Now what? This is where speed and clarity matter.

Start with Clear Communication

Don’t panic—just document. Whip out your phone and start snapping photos of anything that doesn’t match the agreement. Whether it’s undone repairs, missing items, or new damage from the seller’s grand exit, you need evidence.

Then contact the listing agent right away. The faster you flag the problem, the faster the seller can fix it—or at least start groveling. Keep it factual. Notes and photos make sure everyone’s working from the same page.

Explore Options for Resolution

Depending on the issue, you’ve got a few paths forward:

  • Request Repairs: Quick fixes are possible if there’s still time—and the seller’s willing.
  • Ask for Credits or Concessions: Sometimes money off at closing is easier than scrambling to get someone in with a toolbox.
  • Bring in a Contractor: Get quotes for the damage to provide leverage for negotiations.
  • Cancel the contract: whether you can will be dependent on your agreement.

Evaluate the severity. A missing towel rack? Mildly annoying. A flooded basement? That’s gonna need more than a Home Depot run.

Delay Closing If Necessary

Major problems—like safety issues or incomplete repair work—might mean postponing the close. It’s not ideal, but better than taking ownership of a house that doesn’t meet contract terms. The goal here is to ensure the condition of the property aligns with what was promised.

Renegotiate Like a Pro

If the home’s condition doesn’t match what was agreed upon, renegotiating may be the cleanest fix. Adjust the price, ask for closing cost credits, or add a repair escrow to the deal. Go into the conversation with clear priorities—what’s a must, and what’s negotiable?

The ultimate goal? Get your buyer the home they expected without letting issues derail the entire deal. Handling this part like a boss shows you’re not just closing transactions—you’re protecting people. And in the world of final walk through real estate, that’s the difference between a frazzled agent and a trusted one.

Preparing for a Successful Final Walk Through Before Closing

The secret to a smooth walkthrough? Don’t show up empty-handed or wide-eyed. This isn’t the time for a casual stroll to “get a feel” for the place one last time. It’s your buyer’s final property condition check, and they need to walk through like they’re on a mission—because they are.

Coming in prepared helps catch missed repairs, abandoned junk, or any move-out surprises before they become your buyer’s responsibility. That’s where your trusty walkthrough checklist comes in—your personal “catch it now or regret it later” guide.

What to Bring for the Final Property Review

Heading into this last look? Pack smart. Here’s what you’ll want on hand during the pre-closing property review:

  • The purchase contract – to cross-check what was promised vs. what’s actually there
  • A notepad or mobile device – to record any “wait, what is that?” discoveries
  • A camera or phone – for photo evidence (you know, in case that fridge is mysteriously missing)
  • A flashlight – because basements and crawl spaces are never fully lit or fully innocent
  • Inspection report + receipts – to confirm every repair got done, not just “sort of handled”

Going into the final check before closing without these? That’s like showing up to a listing appointment without comps. Be thorough, be organized, and give your buyer peace of mind before they sign the dotted line.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is a final walk through before closing?

A final walk through is the buyer’s last chance to ensure the home is in the agreed-upon condition before closing. It confirms repairs are complete, items are removed, and nothing new is broken.

When does the final walk through happen?

The final walk through usually happens 24 to 48 hours before closing. This gives time to resolve last-minute issues if anything’s out of place.

Who attends the final walk through?

Typically, the buyer and their real estate agent attend the final walk through. Sometimes the seller or an inspector may also be present, depending on the situation.

What should I look for during the final walk through?

During the final walk through, check that all repairs were done, personal items are removed, appliances and systems are working, and no new damage has occurred.

Can a seller refuse a final walk through?

While uncommon, a seller can technically refuse access. However, this raises red flags and may violate terms of the purchase agreement, depending on your contract and state laws.

Does the house need to be empty for the final walk through?

Yes, ideally. An empty house makes it easier to spot damage, check repairs, and ensure nothing was left behind. It also confirms the seller has vacated as agreed.

What happens if problems are found during the final walk through?

If issues are discovered, the buyer can request repairs, financial concessions, or delay closing. Documentation and quick communication with the listing agent are key.
